name: Retail (Zimbabwe)
definition: Businesses that sell goods to final consumers from shops, stores, markets, forecourts, pharmacies or online channels, including mixed wholesale-retail traders.

subSectors.name: General dealers and grocery
subSectors.description: Neighbourhood shops and grocers selling everyday goods.

subSectors.name: Supermarkets and chains
subSectors.description: Multi-branch formal grocery and general merchandise retailers.

subSectors.name: Tuckshops and informal micro-retail
subSectors.description: Very small, often informal outlets and market or street vendors.

subSectors.name: Hardware and building supplies
subSectors.description: Retail of building materials, tools and fittings.

subSectors.name: Pharmacy retail
subSectors.description: Retail of medicines from MCAZ-licensed premises.

subSectors.name: Fuel and LPG retail
subSectors.description: Service stations and LPG retail sites licensed by ZERA.

subSectors.name: Liquor retail
subSectors.description: Bottle stores and other licensed liquor outlets.

subSectors.name: Wholesale-retail hybrids
subSectors.description: Cash-and-carry operations serving both traders and consumers.

typicalBusinessSizes.description: Informal vendors and tuckshops, typically owner-operated.
Single licensed shop with a small staff complement.
Multi-branch or high-volume single-site retailers with formal payroll and VAT registration.
National chains and franchised groups with central procurement and multiple branches.

officialSizeThresholds.note: Zimbabwe's official MSME size classifications (employee, turnover and asset bands) were not verified from an official source in this revision; source the current SME definition before product use.

notes: Descriptive segmentation for product design; not an official statistical classification. Alignment to the ZIMSTAT/ISIC classification is a follow-up research item.
